title: RUSTINS MPBK500 Metal Paint, Black, 500 ml
features: Durable and Weather-Resistant: Rustins Metal Paint provides exceptional durability and weather resistance, ensuring your metal surfaces are protected from harsh environmental elements and maintain their vibrant color over time
Quick Drying Formula: Our advanced quick-dry technology allows for rapid drying times, enabling you to complete your painting projects efficiently with multiple coats in a single day

Multi-Color Options: Available in Gold, White, Silver, Green, and Black, Rustins Metal Paint offers a variety of color choices to match your specific aesthetic preferences and project needs. ADD TO BASKET now
Superior Metal Protection: This high-performance metal paint creates a tough, protective barrier that resists rust, corrosion, and chipping, ensuring long-lasting protection for your metal surfaces.

Easy Application: Designed for smooth and effortless application with a brush or roller, Rustins Metal Paint provides a professional, streak-free finish that enhances the appearance of your metal surfaces.
description: From the manufacturer Rustins Quick Dry Metal Paint Rustins new formula Metal Paint is a low VOC paint for use on both bare and galvanised metal. It can be applied directly without the use of a primer and is suitable for indoor and outdoor use. Ideal for gates, metal tanks and all ferrous metal surfaces. Not suitable for use under cellulose finishes. Containing anti-corrosion additives the paint has a low odour and dries to a smooth satin finish in just 2 hours. Brushes can be cleaned with water. Also available in Gold, Silver & Green in 250ml & 500ml. New improved formulation Water based & quick drying Low odour Satin finish Contains rust inhibitors About Us In 1924 Sidney Rustin opened his first shop in London's Shoreditch district where he sold wood finishing products to local residents and trades people. Sidney's father was a cabinetmaker and his mother funded the business venture. During the second world war the first night raid destroyed the original 1932 factory and the business moved to its present location in Cricklewood, London.
